entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The world's fifth most important cereal, grown for food and feed grain.

entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: A general category for several species of small-grained cereal crops, the world's seventh most important cereal grain.

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions, mainly in Africa and Asia, where the majority of sorghum and millet are grown.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where a large portion of the world's sorghum and millet area is located.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where a large portion of the world's sorghum and millet area is located.

entities.name: LOW RAINFALL
entities.description: An agroecological condition characterized by limited precipitation.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: An agroecological condition characterized by severe water shortage.

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: The artificial application of water to land to enable grain production in unsuitable areas.

entities.name: FOOD
entities.description: A primary utilization purpose for millet and a significant purpose for sorghum.

entities.name: FEED GRAIN
entities.description: A utilization purpose for sorghum, particularly in commercialized systems.

entities.name: INTENSIVE COMMERCIALIZED PRODUCTION
entities.description: A high-input production system characterized by the use of hybrid seeds and fertilizers, mainly for livestock feed.

entities.name: LIVESTOCK FEED
entities.description: The primary output of intensive commercialized sorghum production.

entities.name: DEVELOPED WORLD
entities.description: Regions characterized by intensive, commercialized sorghum production.

entities.name: LATIN AMERICA
entities.description: A region containing both commercialized and low-input sorghum production systems.

entities.name: CARIBBEAN
entities.description: A region containing both commercialized and low-input sorghum production systems.

entities.name: HYBRID SEED
entities.description: A technology used in intensive commercialized sorghum production to increase yields.

entities.name: FERTILIZER
entities.description: A chemical input used to increase crop yields in both commercialized and extensive production systems.

entities.name: IMPROVED WATER MANAGEMENT TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used in commercialized production to optimize water use and increase yields.

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL STOCKFEED MARKETS
entities.description: Markets where a significant portion of commercialized sorghum grain is traded.

entities.name: LOW-INPUT EXTENSIVE PRODUCTION SYSTEMS
entities.description: Production systems characterized by low fertilizer rates and limited technology adoption, mainly for food.

entities.name: IMPROVED VARIETIES
entities.description: Crop varieties being adopted in low-input extensive production systems, particularly in Asia.

entities.name: MOISTURE CONSERVATION TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used to maintain soil moisture, with limited adoption in extensive systems.
